verses.latin_vulgate: Ego flos campi,  et lilium convallium.
verses.king_james: I am the rose of Sharon, and the lily of the valleys.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut lilium inter spinas,  sic amica mea inter filias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּשֽׁוֹשַׁנָּה֙ בֵּ֣ין הַחוֹחִ֔ים כֵּ֥ן רַעְיָתִ֖י בֵּ֥ין הַבָּנֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: As the lily among thorns, so is my love among the daughters.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut malus inter ligna silvarum,  sic dilectus meus inter filios.  Sub umbra illius quem desideraveram sedi,  et fructus ejus dulcis gutturi meo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּתַפּ֙וּחַ֙ בַּעֲצֵ֣י הַיַּ֔עַר כֵּ֥ן דּוֹדִ֖י בֵּ֣ין הַבָּנִ֑ים בְּצִלּוֹ֙ חִמַּ֣דְתִּי וְיָשַׁ֔בְתִּי וּפִרְי֖וֹ מָת֥וֹק לְחִכִּֽי׃
verses.king_james: As the apple tree among the trees of the wood, so is my beloved among the sons. I sat down under his shadow with great delight, and his fruit was sweet to my taste.

verses.latin_vulgate: Introduxit me in cellam vinariam ;  ordinavit in me caritatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֱבִיאַ֙נִי֙ אֶל־בֵּ֣ית הַיָּ֔יִן וְדִגְל֥וֹ עָלַ֖י אַהֲבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: He brought me to the banqueting house, and his banner over me was love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fulcite me floribus,  stipate me malis,  quia amore langueo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סַמְּכ֙וּנִי֙ בָּאֲשִׁישׁ֔וֹת רַפְּד֖וּנִי בַּתַּפּוּחִ֑ים כִּי־חוֹלַ֥ת אַהֲבָ֖ה אָֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Stay me with flagons, comfort me with apples: for I am sick of love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Læva ejus sub capite meo,  et dextera illius amplexabitur me.
verses.king_james: His left hand is under my head, and his right hand doth embrace me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjuro vos, filiæ Jerusalem,  per capreas cervosque camporum,  ne suscitetis, neque evigilare faciatis dilectam,  quoadusque ipsa velit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִשְׁבַּ֨עְתִּי אֶתְכֶ֜ם בְּנ֤וֹת יְרוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ בִּצְבָא֔וֹת א֖וֹ בְּאַיְל֣וֹת הַשָּׂדֶ֑ה אִם־תָּעִ֧ירוּ ׀ וְֽאִם־תְּע֥וֹרְר֛וּ אֶת־הָאַהֲבָ֖ה עַ֥ד שֶׁתֶּחְפָּֽץ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: I charge you, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, by the roes, and by the hinds of the field, that ye stir not up, nor awake my love, till he please.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox dilecti mei ; ecce iste venit,  saliens in montibus, transiliens colles.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֣וֹל דּוֹדִ֔י הִנֵּה־זֶ֖ה בָּ֑א מְדַלֵּג֙ עַל־הֶ֣הָרִ֔ים מְקַפֵּ֖ץ עַל־הַגְּבָעֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: The voice of my beloved! behold, he cometh leaping upon the mountains, skipping upon the hills.

verses.latin_vulgate: Similis est dilectus meus capreæ,  hinnuloque cervorum.  En ipse stat post parietem nostrum,  respiciens per fenestras,  prospiciens per cancellos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּוֹמֶ֤ה דוֹדִי֙ לִצְבִ֔י א֖וֹ לְעֹ֣פֶר הָאַיָּלִ֑ים הִנֵּה־זֶ֤ה עוֹמֵד֙ אַחַ֣ר כׇּתְלֵ֔נוּ מַשְׁגִּ֙יחַ֙ מִן־הַֽחַלֹּנ֔וֹת מֵצִ֖יץ מִן־הַחֲרַכִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: My beloved is like a roe or a young hart: behold, he standeth behind our wall, he looketh forth at the windows, shewing himself through the lattice.

verses.latin_vulgate: En dilectus meus loquitur mihi.     Surge, propera, amica mea,  columba mea, formosa mea, et veni :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנָ֥ה דוֹדִ֖י וְאָ֣מַר לִ֑י ק֥וּמִי לָ֛ךְ רַעְיָתִ֥י יָפָתִ֖י וּלְכִי־לָֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: My beloved spake, and said unto me, Rise up, my love, my fair one, and come away.

verses.latin_vulgate: jam enim hiems transiit ;  imber abiit, et recessit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־הִנֵּ֥ה הַסְּתָ֖ו עָבָ֑ר הַגֶּ֕שֶׁם חָלַ֖ף הָלַ֥ךְ לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For, lo, the winter is past, the rain is over and gone;

verses.latin_vulgate: Flores apparuerunt in terra nostra ;  tempus putationis advenit :  vox turturis audita est in terra nostra ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַנִּצָּנִים֙ נִרְא֣וּ בָאָ֔רֶץ עֵ֥ת הַזָּמִ֖יר הִגִּ֑יעַ וְק֥וֹל הַתּ֖וֹר נִשְׁמַ֥ע בְּאַרְצֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: The flowers appear on the earth; the time of the singing of birds is come, and the voice of the turtle is heard in our land;

verses.latin_vulgate: ficus protulit grossos suos ;  vineæ florentes dederunt odorem suum.  Surge, amica mea, speciosa mea, et veni :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַתְּאֵנָה֙ חָנְטָ֣ה פַגֶּ֔יהָ וְהַגְּפָנִ֥ים ׀ סְמָדַ֖ר נָ֣תְנוּ רֵ֑יחַ ק֥וּמִי (לכי) [לָ֛ךְ] רַעְיָתִ֥י יָפָתִ֖י וּלְכִי־לָֽךְ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The fig tree putteth forth her green figs, and the vines with the tender grape give a good smell. Arise, my love, my fair one, and come away.

verses.latin_vulgate: columba mea, in foraminibus petræ, in caverna maceriæ,  ostende mihi faciem tuam,  sonet vox tua in auribus meis :  vox enim tua dulcis, et facies tua decora.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יוֹנָתִ֞י בְּחַגְוֵ֣י הַסֶּ֗לַע בְּסֵ֙תֶר֙ הַמַּדְרֵגָ֔ה הַרְאִ֙ינִי֙ אֶת־מַרְאַ֔יִךְ הַשְׁמִיעִ֖נִי אֶת־קוֹלֵ֑ךְ כִּֽי־קוֹלֵ֥ךְ עָרֵ֖ב וּמַרְאֵ֥יךְ נָאוֶֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: O my dove, that art in the clefts of the rock, in the secret places of the stairs, let me see thy countenance, let me hear thy voice; for sweet is thy voice, and thy countenance is comely.

verses.latin_vulgate: Capite nobis vulpes parvulas  quæ demoliuntur vineas :  nam vinea nostra floruit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶֽחֱזוּ־לָ֙נוּ֙ שֻֽׁעָלִ֔ים שֻׁעָלִ֥ים קְטַנִּ֖ים מְחַבְּלִ֣ים כְּרָמִ֑ים וּכְרָמֵ֖ינוּ סְמָדַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Take us the foxes, the little foxes, that spoil the vines: for our vines have tender grapes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilectus meus mihi, et ego illi,  qui pascitur inter lilia,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּוֹדִ֥י לִי֙ וַאֲנִ֣י ל֔וֹ הָרֹעֶ֖ה בַּשּׁוֹשַׁנִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: My beloved is mine, and I am his: he feedeth among the lilies.

verses.latin_vulgate: donec aspiret dies, et inclinentur umbræ.  Revertere ; similis esto, dilecte mi, capreæ,  hinnuloque cervorum super montes Bether.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֤ד שֶׁיָּפ֙וּחַ֙ הַיּ֔וֹם וְנָ֖סוּ הַצְּלָלִ֑ים סֹב֩ דְּמֵה־לְךָ֨ דוֹדִ֜י לִצְבִ֗י א֛וֹ לְעֹ֥פֶר הָאַיָּלִ֖ים עַל־הָ֥רֵי בָֽתֶר׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Until the day break, and the shadows flee away, turn, my beloved, and be thou like a roe or a young hart upon the mountains of Bether.
